mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion and rising household income, which drives consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:January 2013 payroll level at 134,021k suggests post-recession recovery momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months do not produce higher average forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near zero (below 0.25%) signals continued monetary accommodation that suppresses discount rates on equities and encourages risk-asset allocation, producing sustained positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of December 2012 indicates zero lower bound regime
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of months when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward returns is positive (contradicting the mechanism).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST and retail sales measured by RSAFS jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth, the combined demand impulse drives corporate profit expectations upward, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS show positive month-over-month growth
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 861k and RSAFS at 415,695M in November 2012 suggest post-crisis demand recovery
regime_hint: demand-led recovery with synchronized housing and consumption growth
failure_condition: Joint signal (both HOUST and RSAFS positive) produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential margin compression, which reduces forward earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.509 in November 2012 near pre-crisis trough levels
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO shocks produce negative 5-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day forward returns is below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 2.5% year-over-year signals monetary policy constraint and real rate compression. When inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, the real rate becomes deeply negative, driving equity valuations higher through duration effects, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS below 0.50%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 231.025 in November 2012 with FEDFUNDS at 0.16% creates negative real rate environment
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Triggered instances (high inflation + low rates) produce positive 60-day returns in fewer than 60% of cases, or the interaction coefficient between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 8.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which dampens discretionary spending and corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.8% in December 2012 near multi-year lows but still elevated
regime_hint: labor market slack with elevated unemployment
failure_condition: Months with UNRATE > 8.0% produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of instances, or correlation between UNRATE level and 20-day forward returns is negative (contradicting the mechanism).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows acceleration above 2.5% annualized rate, signaling robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate exceeds 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,652.5B in Q3 2012 reflects post-crisis recovery trajectory
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% produce positive 60-day forward returns in fewer than 58% of instances, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and subsequent 60-day returns is below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both accelerate month-over-month, signaling synchronized supply and labor expansion that validates demand strength and reduces recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and PAYEMS show positive month-over-month growth simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show recovery momentum in late 2012
regime_hint: synchronized supply-demand expansion with labor market validation
failure_condition: Joint signal (both INDPRO and PAYEMS positive) produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 56% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.
